root_text.sn55.2:1.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo.
root_text.sn55.2:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti:
root_text.sn55.2:2.3: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.sn55.2:2.6: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.2:2.7: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo”ti.
root_text.sn55.2:3.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn55.2:0.3: The Culmination of the Spiritual Life
translation_text.sn55.2:1.1: “Mendicants, a noble disciple who has four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.2:2.2: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.2:2.6: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.2:2.7: A noble disciple who has these four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
translation_text.sn55.2:3.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn55.2:3.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn55.2:4.2: confidence, and vision of the truth,
translation_text.sn55.2:4.4: the culmination of the spiritual life.”
